danger_signals.visual_form: Opponent's health bar reduces in size as damage accumulates
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate threat; opponent approaching defeat or player at risk

danger_signals.visual_form: Visual accumulation of energy during Y and B button hold sequences
danger_signals.meaning: Opponent preparing powerful special move attack

danger_signals.visual_form: Visual morphing and power-up animations specific to each character
danger_signals.meaning: Opponent entering heightened combat capability; escalated danger

danger_signals.visual_form: Character pose and animation startup frames before special attack execution
danger_signals.meaning: Incoming special attack; window for counterattack available

safety_signals.visual_form: Split-screen radar showing character separation; on-screen distance visualization
safety_signals.meaning: Safe spacing from opponent; breathing room to plan strategy

safety_signals.visual_form: Opponent's health bar at maximum length
safety_signals.meaning: Low immediate threat; fight remains early stage or reset

safety_signals.visual_form: Opponent's energy meter empty or minimal
safety_signals.meaning: Opponent cannot execute Ki blast attacks; reduced special move availability

faction_indicators.faction: Cell Arc characters (Cell, Cell Jr., Gohan, Piccolo, Trunks, Vegeta)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Cell's organic biomechanical appearance; Z-Fighter gi variants
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Cell and Cell Jr. display insectoid features; villainous posturing distinguishes them from hero characters
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Tournament arenas and martial arts combat zones establish Cell Games setting context

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Broly's distinctive pale appearance and muscular form; Zangya's unique costume design
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Film antagonists display alien/outsider visual characteristics distinct from main cast
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Water-based arena stages (commissioned to Toei Animation) signal Broly/Bojack film content inclusion

faction_indicators.faction: Z-Fighters core (Goku, Vegeta, Gohan, Trunks, Piccolo)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Standard martial arts gi uniforms; established Dragon Ball character silhouettes
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Vegeta retained as playable despite former villain status; visual design reflects character hierarchy
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Tournament/martial arts themed stages reinforce Z-Fighter combat tradition

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Tournament arena stage design; Cell as central boss opponent; character roster reflects post-Android Saga lineup
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Tournament mode gameplay structure; progression-based fighting matches simulating tournament bracket advancement

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Water-based underwater arena stage; presence of Broly, Zangya, Bojack characters alongside main cast
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Adventure elements in tournament mode; alternative side-story scenarios featuring film antagonists

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Character transformation animations; visual power-up sequences reflecting canonical form changes (Super Saiyan, etc.)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Meteor combo sequences; enhanced special move availability in powered-up states; increased damage output

environmental_storytelling.example: Water-based arenas commissioned from Toei Animation indicate narrative shift toward Broly/Bojack film material; terrain communicates story context
Mix of Cell Arc protagonists and film antagonists (8 playable + 2 unlockable) tells story of combined tournament narrative spanning show and film canon
Character-specific transformation states visually represent power levels and narrative progression; unlockable characters (Goku, Broly) signal story progression
Broly's pale appearance, Zangya's alien design, Cell's biomechanical form visually communicate outsider/threat status within tournament context

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Y and B button holds accumulate energy; Ki gauge fills visually; character assumes charging stance
Button combination input triggers character-specific attack animation; opponent can now counterattack special moves (new mechanic in sequel)
Consecutive attack chains execute automatically after initiation; most characters feature meteor combos (expanded from original game)
Dash back (L button) and dash forward (R button) mechanics; split-screen view adjusts to maintain visibility; radar tracks positions
Adventure mode elements present alternative scenario pathways; these characters signal narrative branches outside main Cell Games tournament

notes: Dragon Ball Z: Super Butōden 2 communicates world-state primarily through character roster presence and arena design. The game lacks explicit faction conflict visualization beyond character-as-opponent paradigm; danger and safety are communicated through mechanical UI (health/Ki bars) and spatial indicators (radar) rather than environmental storytelling. The integration of film antagonists (Broly arc, Bojack arc) alongside Cell Games continuity creates narrative branching visible through stage selection and character availability. Character transformations serve as power-state indicators rather than faction markers. The game's communication model is mechanically-centered rather than environmental; visual storytelling occurs through character design and stage selection rather than dynamic world-state shifts.
sources_used: wikipedia - Dragon Ball Z: Super Butōden 2
